title: cortusa matthioli subsp. moravica
intro: Cortusa matthioli subsp. moravica, also known as the Moravian yellow pimpernel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Primulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with potential applications in treating various health conditions. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess antioxidant, antiviral, and antifungal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and respiratory issues. Cortusa matthioli subsp. moravica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its medicinal properties are also preserved through drying and storage for later use.
